title: Math — Probability
description: PSAT 8/9 probability and conditional probability questions (two-way tables, complements, and proportion-to-count conversions).

questions.prompt: A grove has 6 rows of birch trees and 5 rows of maple trees. Each row of birch trees has 8 trees 20 feet or taller and 6 trees shorter than 20 feet. Each row of maple trees has 9 trees 20 feet or taller and 7 trees shorter than 20 feet. A tree from one of these rows will be selected at random. What is the probability of selecting a maple tree, given that the tree is 20 feet or taller?
questions.explanation: Restrict to trees that are 20 feet or taller. Birch: \(6\times 8=48\). Maple: \(5\times 9=45\). Total: \(48+45=93\). The conditional probability is \(45/93=15/31\).
choiceExplanations: Uses the wrong total, including trees shorter than 20 feet.
Not conditioned on the tree being 20 feet or taller.
Uses an incorrect denominator for the conditional set.

questions.prompt:
Employees at an electric company recorded all calls last Monday and noted whether the caller asked for repairs and whether the caller asked about a bill. The table shows the results:

Asked about a bill: 48 asked for repairs, 623 did not (671 total)
Did not ask about a bill: 130 asked for repairs, 90 did not (220 total)

If a caller who asked about a bill is selected at random, which is closest to the probability that the caller also asked for repairs?
questions.explanation: Condition on callers who asked about a bill. Probability = \(48/671 \approx 0.0715\), which is closest to 0.07.
choiceExplanations: Too small; uses an incorrect numerator or denominator.

questions.prompt: The coat color and eye color for 48 Himalayan kittens are summarized in a table. Cream-tortoiseshell kittens include 16 with deep blue eyes and 16 with light brown eyes. Chocolate kittens include 12 with deep blue eyes and 4 with light brown eyes. What fraction of the chocolate-colored kittens has deep blue eyes?
questions.explanation: Only consider chocolate-colored kittens. Of 16 chocolate kittens, 12 have deep blue eyes. The fraction is \(12/16=3/4\).
choiceExplanations: Uses all kittens instead of only chocolate-colored kittens.
Uses all deep-blue-eyed kittens as the denominator.

questions.prompt: A bag contains 7 red, 4 white, 33 blue, and 33 yellow cubes. If one cube is selected at random, what is the probability the cube is neither blue nor yellow?
questions.explanation: Neither blue nor yellow means red or white. Favorable outcomes: \(7+4=11\). Total cubes: \(77\). Probability = \(11/77=1/7\).

questions.prompt: A table shows 14 cars listed for sale by type and price. Nonhybrid cars include 5 priced at no more than \(\$25{,}000\) and 3 priced greater than \(\$25{,}000\). Hybrid cars include 2 priced at no more than \(\$25{,}000\) and 4 priced greater than \(\$25{,}000\). If one car is selected at random, what is the probability it is a hybrid priced at no more than \(\$25{,}000\)?
questions.explanation: There are 2 hybrid cars priced at no more than \(\$25{,}000\) out of 14 total cars. Probability = \(2/14=1/7\).
